api/client_core.core.Connection.html.title: Class Connection | File-Sharing
api/client_core.core.Connection.html.summary: Class Connection Namespace client_core.core Assembly client-core.dll Class that houses the reader and writer for a connection public class Connection Inheritance object Connection Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Connection object shouldnt be created, rather it should be recieved from the Connector class and used as Connector validates the server To write to connection you add a ProtocolMessage object to the queue through AddTask and the rest is handled Constructors Connection(TcpClient, ILogger) Sets up listening and writing loop for the connection public Connection(TcpClient client, ILogger logger) Parameters client TcpClient TcpClient connection, Ideally should be a server connection that is validated through Connector logger ILogger ILogger that is passed down into here Fields taskQueue public Channel<ProtocolMessage> taskQueue Field Value Channel<ProtocolMessage> Methods AddTask(ProtocolMessage) Puts message into a ordered queue that will serialize messages one at a time public Task AddTask(ProtocolMessage protocolMessage) Parameters protocolMessage ProtocolMessage Message that needs to be sent Returns Task CompleteQueue() Way to end the queue public Task CompleteQueue() Returns Task

api/client_core.core.Connector.html.title: Class Connector | File-Sharing
api/client_core.core.Connector.html.summary: Class Connector Namespace client_core.core Assembly client-core.dll Class has static objests as connector doesnt require object lifecycle as it only server purpose of validating and returning a connection to a server public static class Connector Inheritance object Connector Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Connect(string, int, ILogger) Creates a connection to the server and validates the server, returns a Connection object if it could be validated public static Task<Connection?> Connect(string server, int port, ILogger logger) Parameters server string IP address port int Port number logger ILogger ILogger that was created at initialization Returns Task<Connection> Returns a connection object when a validated connection is estabalished Exceptions TimeoutException When connection takes over more time than timeout was set to, this is thrown Exception Returns exception when server couldn't be validated
